articles.title: Neuro-Agentic Control: A Deep Learning-based LLM-Powered Agentic AI Framework for Controlling Security Controls
articles.summary: The paper proposes a neuro-agentic control framework for industrial IoT cybersecurity that pairs an LLM-based planner with a time-series foundation model to validate actions before they affect operational technology systems. In tests on the Secure Water Treatment dataset, the framework outperformed LSTM and TCN baselines and executed zero physically invalid actions.
articles.advisor_relevance: This is an early research signal that agentic AI may expand into high-stakes industrial control environments if paired with deterministic guardrails and simulation-based validation. While not yet a commercial deployment, it highlights future demand for AI safety layers, industrial cybersecurity, and foundation models tailored to physical systems.

articles.title: Seeing is Free, Speaking is Not: Uncovering the True Energy Bottleneck in Edge VLM Inference
articles.summary: A new arXiv paper profiles energy use for on-device Vision-Language Model inference across five models, multiple resolutions, and NVIDIA RTX 3070 and Jetson Orin NX hardware. The authors find that power draw is relatively constant, while latency and energy are driven primarily by output token generation rather than visual processing, with each output token taking 11 to 39 times longer than each input token. The paper argues that controlling output length can save far more energy than visual token pruning, especially as model size increases.
articles.advisor_relevance: This research is relevant to conversations about the physical and energy constraints of AI deployment at the edge, including robotics and embodied AI. It suggests that optimization efforts may need to shift from reducing visual inputs toward managing model outputs and decoding efficiency, which could influence future edge AI software design and hardware demand. The investment read-through is useful but indirect, as this is an academic paper rather than a commercial deployment or company announcement.

articles.title: 5 Ways Automation is Eliminating Errors at the Shipping Desk
articles.summary: The article explains how shipping automation reduces fulfillment errors by preserving a single shipment record from order through dispatch, validating addresses, capturing package data directly, automating service selection, linking labels to orders, and using final scan verification. The emphasis is on practical workflow automation that reduces manual data entry, improves exception handling, and limits costly mistakes before parcels leave the facility.
articles.advisor_relevance: This is a narrow but practical example of automation improving logistics operations by reducing labor-intensive handoffs, shipping errors, carrier fees, and service mismatches. For advisors, the read-through is that enterprise automation adoption continues to move into routine back-office and fulfillment workflows, though the article does not identify specific companies, AI vendors, or investment catalysts.
